q: In pytest's skipif/xfail evaluation (cached_eval and MarkEvaluator._istrue): what exactly is used as the cache key in cached_eval, where is the cache stored, and which data that affects the evaluation result is NOT part of the key?
facts: cache key is the expression string (expr) alone
cache stored in config._store under evalcache_key
the globals dict (from _getglobals / item.obj.__globals__) is not part of the key

q: In the implementation behind `pytest --fixtures` (_showfixtures_main): which fixtures are skipped at default verbosity, and what exactly is printed (and in which color) when a fixture has no docstring?
facts: fixtures whose argname starts with underscore are skipped unless verbose > 0
'<location>: no docstring available' printed in red

q: In Session.resolve_redirects: which request object is copied at the top of each loop iteration, and which three (status code, method) combinations rewrite the HTTP method to GET?
facts: req.copy() — the original request is copied every iteration, not the previous hop's request
303 see_other with method != HEAD → GET
302 found with method != HEAD → GET
301 moved with method == POST → GET

q: In HTTPAdapter.send: which low-level (urllib3/socket) exceptions are caught, and which requests exception is each re-raised as?
facts: ProtocolError / socket.error → ConnectionError
MaxRetryError → ConnectTimeout (ConnectTimeoutError reason) or RetryError (ResponseError reason) or ConnectionError
_ProxyError → ProxyError
_SSLError → SSLError
ReadTimeoutError → ReadTimeout

q: In Blueprint.add_url_rule (src/flask/blueprints.py): what exactly is asserted about the endpoint and the view function name, and through which call is the actual registration deferred?
facts: assert '.' not in endpoint (Blueprint endpoints should not contain dots)
assert '.' not in view_func.__name__
registration deferred via self.record(lambda s: s.add_url_rule(...))

q: In the `flask routes` command (routes_command in src/flask/cli.py): which columns are printed, which HTTP methods are hidden by default, and which values does --sort accept?
facts: columns Endpoint, Methods, Rule
HEAD and OPTIONS hidden unless --all-methods
--sort accepts endpoint, rule, methods (match is the default order)
